def: Having the quality of preserving from loss, decay, or injury.
See Duck mole, under Duck. [Written also mollingong.]
To cut short; to curtail. [Obs.] Bale.
Pertaining to seaweeds of the genus Laminaria, or to that zoneof the sea (from two to ten fathoms in depth) where the seaweeds ofthis genus grow.
The winding or twining stem of a hop vine or other climbingplant.
Very liberal in giving or bestowing; lavish; as, a munificentbenefactor.-- Mu*nif"i*cent*ly, adv.

A station for taking meteorological observations, makingweather forecasts, or disseminating such information. Such stationsare of the first order when they make observations of all theimportant elements either hourly or by self-registering instruments;of the second order when only important observations are taken; ofthe third order when simpler work is done, as to record rainfall andmaximum and minimum temperatures.
